original: Battle of the Aegates Islands: Romans sink the Carthaginian fleet, ending the First Punic War
Jews are excluded from public office in the Roman Empire
The Giralda minaret designed by architect Ben Ahmad for the Almohad mosque in Seville is completed. Now the Bell Tower for Seville's cathedral.
Spanish Bishop of Panama Tomés de Berlanga discovers the uninhabited Galapagos Islands after his ship drifts off course
England declares war on Spain
Tsar Peter the Great of Russia begins a tour of Western Europe
Spanish army under Don Carlos (III) draws into Naples
An agreement between Nadir Shah and Paul I of Russia is signed near Ganja and Russian troops are withdrawn from Baku
USS Alliance, under Captain Barry, fights and wins the final naval battle of the American Revolutionary War off Cape Canaveral
John Stone of Concord, Massachusetts, patents a pile driver
Pope condemns France's Civil Constitution of the clergy
First official census in Great Britain, revealing a population of approximately 10 million
Karol Kurpiński's opera "Kalmora, or The Paternal Right of the Americans" premieres at the Warsaw Opera
The KNIL also known as the Royal Netherlands East Indies Army is created
The French Foreign Legion is established by King Louis-Philippe to support his war in Algeria
1st money minted in Hawaii
West African political leader El Hadj Umar Tall seizes the city of Ségou, destroying the Bambara Empire of Mali
Great Britain & France recognise independence of Zanzibar
US issues first paper money in the form of $5, $10, $20, $50, $100, $500 and $1000 notes
Red River campaign begun in Louisiana by Union forces
Battle of Monroe's Crossroads, North Carolina
Purdue University (Indiana) admits its 1st student
1st performance of Caesar Franck's symphonic poem for chorus and orchestra "Psyché"
Almon Brown Strowger, an undertaker in Topeka, Kansas, patents the strowger switch, a device which led to the automation of telephone circuit switching
Ivory Coast becomes a French colony (Cote d'Ivoire)
New Mexico State University cancels its 1st graduation ceremony; its only graduand Sam Steele was robbed & killed the night before
Bronx acquires O'Brien Square
Battle of Driefontein, South Africa, British offensive against the Boers, who are forced to withdraw

Regents for the King of Uganda and leading chiefs sign a treaty with Great Britain agreeing to the organization of the government, taxation, courts, military, and other functions of their country, which is under British protection.
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Shamrocks outclass Halifax Crescents, 11-0 to sweep challenge series, 2-0
Earthquake destroys Turkish city of Tochangri
Harry Gammeter of Cleveland patents multigraph duplicating machine
Japanese Army captures Mukden (Shenyang)
Baker Street & Waterloo Railway opens, constructed by the Underground Electric Railways Company of London. The contraction Bakerloo became the official name in July 1906.
Europe's worst mining accident when a coal dust explosion kills 1,060 at Courrieres, France
Pittsburgh Courier begins publishing
Republic of China officially abolishes slavery
Stanley Cup, Quebec Skating Rink, Quebec City, Quebec: Quebec Bulldogs retain trophy; defeat Sydney Millionaires (NS), 6-2 for a 2-0 sweep of the challenge series
William Knox becomes 1st in American Bowling Congress to bowl 300
Suffragettes in London damage Rokeby's painting Venus of Velasquez
British Army captures Neuve Chapelle, Belgium
Batangas was formally founded as one of the Philippines's earliest encomiendas
Home Rule Act is passed by the British Parliament, dividing Ireland into two parts; it is rejected by the southern counties, where the Anglo-Irish War continues for a year
Karl Hjalmar Branting becomes the first Social Democrat Prime Minister of Sweden
KLZ-AM in Denver CO begins radio transmissions
State of siege proclaimed during mine strike Johannesburg, South Africa
Cyprus becomes a British crown colony
Run on Belgian banks
Albania mobilizes due to threats from the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes
Christine Collins' son, Walter Collins, disappears in Los Angeles
Major earthquake (6.4 magnitude) in Long Beach, California kills 120, and destroys 70 school buildings
Nevada becomes 1st US state to regulate narcotics
Longest undefeated streak in Toronto Maple Leaf history ends - 18 games with 15 wins, 3 ties
17 villages damaged by hailstones in Hyderabad, India
1st US opera telecast, W2XBS, NYC: Ruggero Leoncavallo's "Pagliacci"
Lee MacPhail, Dodger GM predicts all players will wear batting helmets
U-575 sinks British corvette HMS Asphodel in the Atlantic Ocean killing 92 of the 97 men aboard
Germany blows up Wessel Bridge on Rhine
Japan grants occupied Vietnam independence
US troops land in western Mindanao, Philippines in Operation VICTOR IV
Train derailment kills 185 near Aracaju, northeast Brazil
1st civilian to exceed speed of sound - Herb H Hoover, Edwards Air Force Base, California
General strike in Cyprus protesting exile of Archbishop Makarios
Peter Twiss sets new world air record 1,132 mph (1,823 kph)
Thousands of soccer fans riot in Italy
Tibetan uprising against Chinese occupation force in Lhasa, Tibet
USSR agrees to stop nuclear testing
Due to its no black policy, Phillies leave Jack Tar Harrison Hotel & move to Rocky Point Motel, 20 miles outside Clearwater, Florida
Gala preview dinner held atop the Space Needle in Seattle, Washington, prior to opening of the Century 21 Exposition (World's Fair)
Simon and Garfunkel record the first version of "The Sound of Silence" at Columbia Studios in New York City
US reconnaissance plane shot down over East Germany
"I've Got a Tiger By the Tail" album by Buck Owens is released (Billboard Album of the Year, 1965)
Dutch Princess Margriet & Pieter van Vollenhoven get engaged
5 time American Horse of the Year, Kelso, retires from racing
North Vietnamese capture US Green Beret Camp at Ashau Valley
North Vietnamese and communist Laotion troops overrun a secret US radar facility, Lima Site 85, on a Laos mountaintop
Members of the Stormont Parliament of Northern Ireland given police protection
South Africa cricket team completes a 4-0 series drubbing of Australia in South Africa in the country's last official international cricket Test for 22 years
Three members of the Royal Highland Fusiliers (a regiment of the British Army) are killed by members of the Irish Republican Army
US Senate approves amendment lowering voting age to 18
"What's Up, Doc?", Peter Bogdanovich's film homage to screwball comedies, starringBarbra Streisand, Ryan O'Neal, Madeline Kahn & Kenneth Mars premieres
1st black US political convention opens in Gary, Indiana
General Lon Nol becomes President & Prince Sirik Matak premier of Cambodia
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Morocco adopts constitution
Christian Democrats win Belgium parliamentary election
Lawrence Rowe completes 302 v Eng Bridgetown, 36 fours 1 six
Dog spectacles patented in England
Sanyo Shinkansen open between Osaka and Fukuoka.
CBS' premiere of "A Circle of Children", starring Jane Alexander and based on the life and book about this by Mary Mac Cracken
Rings of Uranus discovered during occulation of SAO
Soyuz 28 returns to Earth
Eubie Blake, a 92-year old ragtime piano player and composer, and Gregory Hines are musical guest on Saturday Night Live
Willard Scott becomes the weather forcaster on the Today Show
"Bette Davis Eyes" single released by Kim Carnes (Billboard Song of the Year 1981)
Saleem Malik scores 100 in 2nd inning of Test Cricket debut (v SL)
Syzygy: all 9 planets aligned on same side of Sun
The United States places an embargo on Libyan petroleum imports because of their support of terrorist groups
Travis Jackson and former commissioner Happy Chandler elected to baseball's Hall of Fame
Heavyweight Tim Witherspoon KOs Greg Page
Dallas Maverick coach Dick Motta is 4th NBA coach to win 700 games
French socialists lose election (National Front 9%)
Ice Pairs Championship at Tokyo won by Elena Valova and Oleg Vasiliev (Soviet Union)
India beat Pakistan to win "World Championship of Cricket"
Vatican formal opposition to test-tube fertilization & embryo transfer
Ice Dance Championship at Halifax won by Klimova & Ponomarenko (Soviet Union)
Ice Pairs World Championship at Halifax won by Ekaterina Gordeeva & Sergei Grinkov (USSR)
Ladies Figure Skating Champions in Halifax won by Jill Trenary (USA)
Prosper Avril resigns as President of Haiti
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Eddie Sutton is 1st NCAA coach to lead 4 schools into playoffs
Marc Girardelli of Luxembourg clinches his 4th slalom World Cup
Merlene Ottey runs world record 200 m indoor (22.24 sec)
Rico Lieder, Jens Carlowitz, Karsten Just and Thomas Schonlebe walk 4x400m indoor world record (3:03.05)
"Come and Talk to Me" single released by Jodeci (Billboard Song of the Year 1992)
Sandra Seuser, Katrin Schreiter, Annet Hesselbarth and Grit Breuer walk female indoor world record 4x400m (3:27.22)
Physician David Gunn shot and killed by anti-abortionist Michael Frederick Griffin in Pensacola, Florida, first anti-abortion murder of a doctor in the US
1 million Greeks attend actress and activist Melina Mercouri's funeral
Car bomb explodes in Karachi at shiite mosque, 17+ killed
Chiel Meijering's "St Louis Blues" premieres in Arnhem
NYC Mayor Rudy Guiliani visits Israel
NASDAQ Composite stock market index peaks at 5132.52, signaling the beginning of the end of the dot-com boom
"My Front Porch Looking In" single released by Lonestar (Billboard Song of the Year 2003)
Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter arrives at Mars
Mass unrest by the PCC crime syndicate in São Paulo, Brazil, eventually kills more than 152 people
At least 130 rockets are fired into Israel from Gaza
Twelfth Republican presidential candidates debate hosted by CNN, held in Coral Gables, Florida
16 people die after being struck by lightning at a church in Nyaruguru District, Rwanda
Two girls aged 8 and 6 become the first female weightlifters to appear on Iranian television after a protest
Ethiopian Airlines flight ET302 crashes just after take-off from Addis Ababa, killing all 157 on board
Taliban force attacks Afghan army base killing or capturing about 50 soldiers in Badghis Province, Afghanistan
Video gaming platform Roblox goes public on the New York Stock Exchange, valued at $45 billion
After a 99-day lockout, Major League Baseball and MLB Players Association reach a new collective bargaining agreement; MLB teams set to play full 162 game season in 2022
True global death toll from COVID-19 estimated at 18.2 million in new study by Washington University
Arch rivals Iran and Saudi Arabia agree to re-establish diplomatic ties between them in a surprise move, after talks in Beijing
California's Silicon Valley Bank, the main bank for tech-start ups,  collapses after a sudden bank run and credit crisis - largest US bank fail since 2008

Gary Lineker, the BBC’s highest-paid presenter, suspended for tweeting UK government's immigration policy a "immeasurably cruel policy directed at the most vulnerable people in language that is not dissimilar to that used by Germany in the 30s”.
Researchers in Singapore name newly discovered cockroach after

Thai health authorities report 1.3 million people have suffered air pollution-related diseases in 2023 in northern Thailand due to smog nearly 125 times WHO standards from farm burning and forest fires
Xi Jinpingappointed to a historic third term as President of China (previously restricted to two terms)
First pig liver transplanted into a clinically dead human for 10 days, in milestone test for animal organ transplants into people, at Xijing Hospital, Xi’an, China
Collision in the North Sea off the coast of England between a cargo ship and a tanker carrying jet fuel which catches fire, killing one person
Queen Elizabeth Iof England gives Johan Casimir £20,000 to aid Dutch rebellion
English KingCharles Idissolves Parliament for the 4th time in his reign, summons new Parliament 11 years later, only to be dissolved after 3 months
KingLouis XIVbegins his personal rule of France, after the death of his chief minister, Cardinal Mazarin

French Huguenot Jean Calas, who was wrongly convicted of killing his son, dies after being tortured by authorities; the event inspiresVoltaireto begin campaign for religious tolerance and legal reform
Abraham Lincolnapplies for a patent (only US President to do so) for a device to lift a boat over shoals and obstructions
Heavyweight Boxing champJohn L. Sullivandraws Charlie Mitchell in 30 rounds
A United States court of appeals rules thatThomas Edisondid not invent the movie camera
1st performance ofMaurice Ravel's "Sonatine" by pianist Paule de Lestang, in Lyon, France
NHL's Quebec BulldogJoe Malonescores 6 goals vs Ottawa Senators
Walter Mittelholzeris 1st to fly over Mount Demawend in Iran
Bavaria lifts ban onAdolf Hitler's speeches
Oswald Mosleyleaves British Labour party and founds the "New Party"

Benny Goodmanunofficially crowned the “King of Swing” at New York City's Paramount Theater as his band plays before exuberant sell-out crowd filled with teenagers; unable to attend nightclub performances, kids dance in the aisles
"Jezebel" film premieres directed byWilliam Wyler, starringBette DavisandHenry Fonda, from the play by Owen Davis
10th Academy Awards: "The Life ofÉmile Zola",Spencer Tracy, andLuise Rainerwin

Tennessee Williams' "Sweet Bird of Youth" premieres in NYC

SF Warriors centerWilt Chamberlainscores 70 points in 163-148 defeat to Syracuse Nationals at Onondaga War Memorial

New York governorAndrew Cuomodeploys the national guard to New Rochelle after one-mile radius zone established as 108 cases of COVID-19 detected
Russian lower house of Parliament passes legislation to allowVladimir Putinto hold office of President for life

Three months into the COVID-19 epidemic Chinese PresidentXi Jinpingfinally travels to Wuhan, epicenter of the outbreak as the rate of daily new infections declines in China falls to 19 new cases and 17 deaths

96th Academy Awards: Best Film "Oppenheimer"; Best DirectorChristopher Nolan, acting Cillian Murphy,Emma Stone,Robert Downey Jr, Da'vine Joy Randolph;Ryan Goslingsings "I'm Just Ken"
